title: Tephroseris polycephala
taxonomy: Tephroseris polycephala is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics, such as its flower structure and chromosome number. Tephroseris polycephala is further grouped into the subfamily Carduoideae and the tribe Cardueae. Within the Cardueae tribe, it is placed in the genus Tephroseris, which contains several species of daisies and related plants. The classification of Tephroseris polycephala provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family.
uses: Tephroseris polycephala, also known as the many-headed aster, is a plant species that has been utilized in traditional medicine in various regions.
